demographics.living_situation: lives at home with his mother, who is present

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I can't breathe, my chest is crushing me, and I just can't look like this or be around people anymore.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: He has been panicking for hours and can't calm down, and his mother is scared for him and rang for help.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 5 hours ago, building up before a social event he was dreading
symptom_narrative.duration: roughly 5 hours and it hasn't let up
symptom_narrative.character: chest feels tight and crushing, can't get a proper breath, heart pounding, shaking, feeling like something terrible is about to happen
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: he says it is unbearable and he cannot cope
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: chest and throat, and a whole-body sense of dread
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started, comes in waves that get worse
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: thinking about how he looks and about being around other people
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: trembling, breathing very fast, feeling weak all over, tearful, does not want to be seen
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he says he has felt very anxious in social situations before but never this bad or this long
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: cannot leave his room, cannot face the social event, mother had to make the call for him

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: I have a genetic condition called Klinefelter syndrome (47 XXY) that I've had since birth
past_medical_history.how_managed: specialist follow-up and a hormone (testosterone) gel

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: I'm very tall with long arms and legs, I have very little body and facial hair, and I've got some breast tissue that's been getting worse, which I hate

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: 19-year-old living with his mother; very shy and socially withdrawn; stopped his prescribed testosterone gel about 3 weeks ago because he was frustrated it wasn't changing his body fast enough; his room is dark and he has been isolating

communication_style.typical_message_style: short, distressed messages; his mother often speaks for him

reluctance_topics: thoughts about not wanting to be here

objective_data_offered.value: he's breathing really fast, I counted about 26 breaths in a minute
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: counted by his mother, not a device

objective_data_offered.value: he's trembling, hyperventilating, tearful and won't make eye contact
he says his heart is pounding and racing
